phases.goal: Identify your biggest time drains and define your most important work

steps.title: Run a time audit
steps.description: Track every activity in 30-minute blocks for 5 days. Most people are shocked to discover how little time goes to their most important work. The average knowledge worker spends less than 3 hours per day on deep, focused work.
steps.success_metric: 5-day time audit completed and top 3 time drains identified

steps.title: Identify your MIT (Most Important Tasks)
steps.description: Each morning, identify the one task that — if completed — would make the day a success regardless of everything else. This is your MIT. Do it first, before email, before meetings. Everything else is secondary.
steps.success_metric: Daily MIT identified and completed first for 10 consecutive days

phases.goal: Build reliable systems for capture, focus, and task management

steps.title: Build a trusted capture system
steps.description: Your brain is for thinking, not storing. Write down every task, idea, and obligation as it arises — in one trusted place (Notion, Obsidian, paper). A full brain dump at the start of each week clears mental overhead and reduces the anxiety of forgetting.
steps.success_metric: Single trusted capture system set up and used for 2 weeks

steps.title: Create protected deep work blocks
steps.description: Schedule 2-hour blocks of uninterrupted work in your calendar. Close email and Slack. Set your phone to Do Not Disturb. Tell your team. Deep work is rare and valuable — it can't happen in the gaps between notifications.
steps.success_metric: 2-hour deep work blocks protected 3+ times per week for 4 weeks

steps.title: Implement a weekly review
steps.description: Every Friday or Sunday: review what you completed, what's outstanding, what's coming next week, and reset your task list. This 30-minute review prevents urgent tasks from crowding out important ones and keeps you operating strategically.
steps.success_metric: Weekly review completed for 4 consecutive weeks

phases.goal: Eliminate the last major productivity leaks

steps.title: Batch and eliminate low-value tasks
steps.description: Identify recurring tasks that can be batched (email twice a day, not constantly), delegated, or eliminated entirely. Every task you remove from your list is a permanent productivity gain. Say no more — every yes is a no to something else.
steps.success_metric: At least 3 low-value tasks batched, delegated, or eliminated

steps.title: Design your environment for focus
steps.description: Remove phone from desk during deep work. Use a separate browser profile for work. Use website blockers during focus sessions. Add friction to distracting apps. Add ease to focused work tools. Environment design beats willpower every time.
steps.success_metric: Physical and digital environment redesigned to support deep work
